# AFM-0005. Marker-Protected Safe-Write for Generated Files

Date: 2026-04-27
Last-reviewed: 2026-04-28
Tier: B
Status: Accepted

## Related

References: AFM-0001

## Context

`adr-fmt` regenerates README index files on every invocation. If a
developer hand-edits a README, the next run would silently destroy
those edits. Three strategies exist: never overwrite (stale indexes
accumulate), always overwrite (destroys hand-edits), or
marker-protected overwrite (check for a sentinel comment, only
overwrite files carrying the marker, skip others with a warning).

## Decision

Use an HTML comment marker on the first line of generated files.
Only overwrite files whose first line matches the marker exactly.

- **R1**: Generated files carry
  `<!-- Generated by adr-fmt. Do not edit. -->` as the first line
- **R2**: Only overwrite files whose first line matches the marker
  exactly; skip files without the marker and emit a warning
- **R3**: Create new files with the marker on line 1 if the file
  does not yet exist
- **R4**: File writes use `std::fs::write()` without atomic
  semantics; idempotent output makes interrupted writes
  self-correcting on the next run

## Consequences

Developers can create hand-edited README files that `adr-fmt` will
never touch. The marker convention is documented in `--guidelines`.
Generated READMEs are committed to version control for GitHub web
UI navigation. The skip-with-warning behavior is informational, not
blocking (per AFM-0003).
